meaning: vi.转向,(风向)顺(时针)转vt.使转向,放出(锚)n.转向,方向的转变
examples: He is unlikely to veer from his boss's strongly held views.
The ocean storm seems to veer away from you.
But the ECB is unlikely to veer from its mission for long.
While nearly every hotel in town offers an iftar, the dishes tend to veer towards the traditional.
Meeting with Van der Veer in the Hague last November, Putin reportedly criticized the cost escalation.
